ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department providing support */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject related to messaging */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the subject */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "messages" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user sending the message */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject of the message */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the message */,
  "msg_send_date" TEXT /* Date and time the message was sent */,
  "replied" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the message has been replied to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user sending the message */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department related to the message */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subject related to the message */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the message (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_messages_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_messages_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_messages_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "replies" (
  "reply_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reply */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* ID of the message being replied to */,
  "reply_content" TEXT /* Content of the reply */,
  "reply_date" TEXT /* Date and time the reply was made */,
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the agent making the reply */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reply_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_message_id FOREIGN KEY ("message_id") REFERENCES messages ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_agent_id FOREIGN KEY ("agent_id") REFERENCES agents ("agent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "status_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* ID of the message related to the log entry */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the message (e.g., Open, Closed) */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the status was logged */,
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the agent updating the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_status_logs_message_id FOREIGN KEY ("message_id") REFERENCES messages ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_status_logs_agent_id FOREIGN KEY ("agent_id") REFERENCES agents ("agent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attachments" (
  "attachment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attachment */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* ID of the message the attachment relates to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the attachment */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the attachment was uploaded */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the attachment file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attachment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attachments_message_id FOREIGN KEY ("message_id") REFERENCES messages ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "agents" (
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each agent */,
  "agent_name" TEXT /* Full name of the agent */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the agent */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the agent */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the agent belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("agent_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_agents_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "priorities" (
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each priority level */,
  "priority_name" TEXT /* Name of the priority level (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the priority level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("priority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* ID of the message related to the analytics entry */,
  "response_time" INTEGER /* Response time for the message (in minutes) */,
  "user_satisfaction" TEXT /* User satisfaction rating (e.g., Good, Neutral, Bad) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_message_id FOREIGN KEY ("message_id") REFERENCES messages ("id")
)
